channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump hosts Jordan's king and renews insistence that the US can control and redevelop Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump hosted Jordan’s King Abdullah II at the White House on Tuesday and renewed his insistence that Gaza could be emptied of all residents, controlled by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Salman Rushdie testifies about his shock and pain as a stranger repeatedly stabbed him on stage
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Salman Rushdie described in graphic detail Tuesday the frenzied moments in 2022 when a masked man rushed at him on a stage in western New York and repeatedly slashed him with a knife, leaving him with terrible injuries and fearful he would die.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge leaves intact a ban on DOGE access to Treasury records pending a hearing Friday
item.description: A federal judge has left intact a ban that prevents Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department records containing sensitive personal data for millions of Americans, though she made clear that Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is exempt.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DOGE cuts $900 million from agency that tracks American students' academic progress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal research office that tracks the progress of America’s students is being hit with almost $900 million in cuts after Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency found no need for much of its work.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Video shows speeding jet owned by Mötley Crüe's Vince Neil plow into parked plane
item.description: Dramatic video has been released showing the moment a private jet owned by Vince Neil – the lead singer of Mötley Crüe – veer off a runway in Scottsdale Airport in Arizona and smash into a parked plane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kris Bowers talks Oscars nom for 'Wild Robot,' John Williams' impact and Kobe Bryant's advice
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Kris Bowers embraced a new challenge with his first animated film, turning his signature storytelling through music into an Oscar-nominated masterpiece.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Good Samaritan helps deputies capture suspect in foot chase in Denver
item.description: “There was a car on its rims, it didn’t have tires and there was a lot of fire coming from the rims,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Nate Belt, NewsNation

item.title: Punk rock pioneer battling stage 4 cancer and brain tumor; family asks for help
item.description: His family is holding a fundraiser to help pay for a nurse, physical therapy, and "day-to-day vital living expenses.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Canned tuna sold at Walmart, Trader Joe's, Costco, others is recalled in multiple states
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a recall on canned tuna products sold at several different retailers including Walmart, Trader Joe's, Kroger, and Costco.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Storm makes for treacherous travel as snow and ice track through the mid-Atlantic
item.description: A storm system bore down Tuesday on the mid-Atlantic states, covering roads with snow and ice and leading to school closures and worries about possible power outages.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PBS closes DEI office to comply with executive order
item.description: PBS' move comes as Trump's FCC also continues to turn up the heat on public broadcasters over taxpayer funding and editorial direction.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: As egg prices continue to soar, grocers like Trader Joe's limit how many cartons customers can buy
item.description: Trader Joe’s and other grocers are limiting the number of eggs customers can buy across the U.S., citing limited supply caused by the ongoing bird flu outbreak.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dogs from great Danes to terriers head into the ring at Westminster show
item.description: Big dogs are having their day at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show, where a bunch of massive breeds are being judged Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York mayor vows to regain public's trust after Justice Department orders halt to prosecution
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams thanked the Justice Department for ordering a halt to his criminal corruption case and castigated the prosecution as “cruel” while vowing to regain the trust of voters ahead of his upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz And Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eric Adams reiterates innocence amid DOJ order
item.description: The Justice Department ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against the New York City mayor.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump seeks to expand his influence over arts and culture with Kennedy Center moves
item.description: After dominating U.S. politics for nearly a decade, President Trump now appears to be setting his sights on the country’s cultural landscape.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s proposal for Gaza entangles Arab allies
item.description: President Trump’s proposal to permanently resettle Palestinians from the Gaza Strip is complicating relationships with allies in the Arab world.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Villagers offer support for a critical lifeline in rural Alaska days after plane crash kills 10
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The small, striped plane rolled to a stop on a snow-covered airstrip in a remote western Alaska village not far from where a crash just days earlier had killed all 10 people on board.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump warns of ending Israel-Hamas ceasefire if all hostages aren't returned
item.description: President Trump on Monday suggested he might end a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas if all hostages being held in Gaza are not released by the end of the week, warning that if the deal does not go through, “all hell is going to break out.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Your student loans won't be forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos are claiming otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: FEMA says it's halting payments for migrant housing in New York after Musk blasts money for hotels
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The acting head of the federal agency responsible for responding to disasters said Monday that he’s suspending payments sent to New York City to house migrants and that staff who made them will be held accountable, after Elon Musk blasted the transactions on his social media platfo
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Person killed in Union Station shooting during rush hour, police search for suspect
item.description: WASHINGTON (DC News Now) -- A person was shot and killed during rush hour Monday afternoon at D.C.'s Union Station. The United States Capitol Police (USCP) said officers responded to Union Station at around 4:40 p.m., where they found one person who had been shot.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gable, NewsNation

item.title: Confusion after emergency alert sent to phones in San Francisco
item.description: (KRON) -- Some cell phone users in San Francisco received an emergency alert Monday afternoon. Phones sounded the emergency alert tone after a notification said that an endangered adult was missing.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: HIV infections could jump over 6 times if US support dropped & not replaced, UNAIDS chief says
item.description: The head of the U.N. AIDS agency said Monday the number of new HIV infections could jump more than six times by 2029 if American support of the biggest AIDS program is dropped, warning that millions of people could die and more resistant strains of the disease could emerge.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG AP Medical Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Super Bowl LIX highlights: Memorable plays, moving tributes and a historic presidential visit
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in the first Super Bowl to be held in New Orleans in a dozen years. It was played amid heavy security — and with President Donald Trump in attendance — just 41 days after a man plowed a truck into New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, KRON4

item.title: Super Bowl halftime performer detained after unfurling Sudanese-Palestinian flag
item.description: A performer in Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime show was detained late Sunday after showing a Sudanese-Palestinian flag on the field.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he is serious about Canada becoming 51st state in Super Bowl interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is serious about wanting Canada to become the 51st state in an interview that aired Sunday during the Super Bowl preshow.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Marine from California identified as one of 4 people killed in Philippines plane crash
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (AP) — A U.S. Marine from California was identified by military officials Sunday as one of four people killed when a plane contracted by the U.S. military crashed in a rice field in the southern Philippines. Sgt. Jacob M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said. The Nome Volunteer Fire Department made the announcement on its Facebook page Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Elon Musk is calling for the impeachment of the federal judge who made a decision early Saturday morning that the Treasury Department should block access to anyone “other than civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties” from its payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KRON4
